title: About the Central Government Enterprise Survey | Stats NZ
content:
## About the Central Government Enterprise Survey
21 September 2023, 10:45am
Information about taking part in the Central Government Enterprise Survey, such as why we carry out the survey, how we selected you, and how to complete the questionnaire.
The purpose of the Central Government Enterprise Survey is to collect data on the financial transactions of Crown entities.
The financial data collected by this survey is needed to compile quarterly and annual statistics on the performance of the New Zealand economy, such as calculating gross domestic product (GDP) and statistics on Crown research institutes.
The following Crown entities are selected to participate in the survey:
- Crown research institutes
- Maritime Safety Authority
- Civil Aviation Authority (Aviation Safety and Aviation Security Service).
The questions included in this survey are being asked under the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Our approach to survey compliance has more information about your legal obligation to participate in the Central Government Enterprise Survey.
We carry out the survey every quarter.

## How to complete the survey
You fill in a paper or electronic (Excel) questionnaire.
- Contact us for survey help 26 May 2020
- Business survey information 31 July 2017
- Privacy, security, and confidentiality of survey data 20 May 2026
- Government finance 01 January 2017 Image: behive building
